characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Band of Relatives: They're a three-member underground rock band comprised of three siblings. Deadpan Snarker: All three of the hedgehogs have some snarky comments in them. Sonic mostly does the snarking, but Sonia - and to a lesser extent Manic - have their moments. Sibling Team: The hedgehogs are triplets, and are pretty much the leaders of the resistance against Robotnik's regime.

characters.name: Sonic the Hedgehog
characters.description: The protagonist and oldest of the royal triplets, Sonic was raised in a middle-class home. He is a super-fast hedgehog capable of exceeding the speed of sound, and plays a magic electric guitar that also works as a laser gun. Despite his wisecracking personality, Sonic has a strong sense of justice. Ambiguously Related: Like inSatAM, he refers to Chuck as "uncle", and the two look very similar, but since in this continuity Sonic was adopted and knew Chuck before his own birth mother, whether they are still blood relatives in this series is unclear. Beware the Silly Ones: He's the least focused and (usually) the least serious of the three, but also the one who wreaks the most havoc with Robotnik's plans. Big Eater: As with most western adaptations, he is usually seen scarfing down on many chili dogs. Is the Answer to This Question "Yes"?:He once answered a question with "Are chili dogs a basic food group?".In another episode, he answers a question with "Is Robotnik round?".

characters.name: Sonia the Hedgehog
characters.description: The middle child of the triplets, Sonia was raised by the aristocracy and can be pretty full of herself at times. She plays a magical keyboard that doubles as a laser gun. '80s Hair: She basically has a pink mohawk. Ace Pilot: She proves to have amazing pilot skills in episode 15. Badass Biker: Rides a pink-and-purple motorbike, and is quite skilled at driving it. It gets destroyed in one episode after crashing into a wall. Bound and Gagged: In "Dunes Day", after discovering thatIfyoucan was roboticized.

characters.name: Manic the Hedgehog
characters.description: Manic is the youngest of the triplets, and was raised in the slums by a gang of thieves. He's easily the most laid-back of his siblings, and plays a magic set of drums that can cause powerful shockwaves. Anime Hair: Arguably the most wild of anySoniccharacter: it's green and sticks up all over the place at odd angles. The Artful Dodger: Was raised by thieves (who stole him from a doorstop, no less), and has trouble packing the habit in after reuniting with his siblings. Badass Adorable: Wears his heart on his sleeve a lot more than Sonic, and almost as much as Sonia. Badass Normal: While Sonic has hisSuper-Speed, and Sonia has herSuper-Strength, Manic only has his lock-picking ability and hisHover Boardto get him by.

characters.name: Queen Aleena
characters.description: The former queen of Mobius and mother of the royal triplets, Aleena was usurped from her throne and is living in hiding until the prophesied moment, occasionally helping her children from afar. Action Mom: Occasionally, most prominently in "Sonia's Choice" whereshe flies in piloting afreaking gunship, destroys several SWAT-bots, and rescues Manic. Badass Boast: Gives a really rousing one to Robotnik in "Wedding Bell Blues".Queen Aleena:"You are a blight upon our land, Robotnik! I, Queen Aleena, shallneverabandon Mobius or her children as long as I may live!" Big Good: Though unable to aid her children directly, Aleena does her best to guide them and give them help in their times of need. The Chessmaster: She always seems to be pulling strings in the background to get the hedgehogs to the right place at the right time.

characters.name: Dr. Robotnik
characters.description: Dr. Robotnik is the evil dictator of Mobius, and the boss of Sleet and Dingo. Having already conquered the whole planet with his technology and intelligence, he seeks to remove Queen Aleena and the Hedgehogs from the picture to end any opposition to his rule. Adaptational Badass: Despite the occasional moments where things go badly wrong for him or jokes are made at his expense, this is still one of Robotnik's most competent and sinister portrayals. He rules the entire planet Mobius with an iron fist and is an ever-present threat to the hedgehogs, to the point that in "The Art of Destruction", Manic starts to contemplate whether there's even any point in trying to fight him. Adaptational Dye-Job: His design is very similar to hisSatAMself, but with the addition of a long gray sleeve on his right arm to match his roboticized left one, and a long red cape instead of a short yellow one. Adaptational Villainy: He might have the occasional funny moment or two, sure, but this version of the egg-shaped scientist is just as pure evil as hisSatAMcounterpart and is completely devoid of the sympathetic, redeemable, or likable traits that his game counterpart has. Aristocrats Are Evil: Robotnik gained the footing for his takeover by appealing to the upper-crust of Mobius and reassuring them that they would be able to keep their cushy lifestyles if they allowed him to rule.

characters.name: Sleet
characters.description: The intelligent wolf minion of Dr. Robotnik and partner in crime of Dingo. He, along with his partner, are a pair of bounty hunters tasked with capturing Sonic and his siblings. Anime Hair: Blue and spiky. Beware the Silly Ones: Sleet is subjected to a lot of slapstick, but remains a threatening villain and the Robotnik Empire's chief enforcer. For example, in "Who Do You Think You Are", Sleet takes part in a silly sequence where he is entertained by Sonic and Manic disguised asSultry Belly Dancers, but then later almost roboticizes Sonia and partially succeeds in roboticizing her friend Abdul. He's also the only character with a confirmed body count, as ART can attest. Character Development: Sleet originally worked for Robotnik out of fear, but over time he came to love the power and ability to hurt people that came with his position in the Robotnik empire. In "The Art of Destruction", when Robotnik allows him to quit working for him due to his new lackey, ART, Sleet is incensed and angered about the idea of losing his position in the Robotnik Empire. Later in the episode, Sleet is overjoyed when ART turns traitor and takes personal enjoyment in killing the robot. Chronic Backstabbing Disorder:Sleet has backstabbed Robotnik on several occasions. In "New Echidna in Town", after betraying Robotnik and being caught, Sleet begs for mercy and the moment Robotnik decides to give him another chance, Sleet is already planning about obtaining the Chaos Emerald and taking over Mobius himself.Sleetregularly roboticizes informers the moment they're no longer useful to him.Many episodes establish that Sleet is embezzling Dingo's payment and plans to have him killed after the Sonic Underground is finished just to get Dingo's half of the reward money from Robotnik.

characters.name: Dingo
characters.description: The dim-witted minion of Dr. Robotnik and partner in crime of Sleet. He can shapeshift using a remote. Abhorrent Admirer: Dingo has a big crush on Sonia, who doesn't feel the same way and, in fact, understandably hates him. Affably Evil: Dingo seems largely unaware of what is going on most of the time, and just seems to be along for the ride. Almighty Idiot: At one point, Dingo absorbs a Chaos Emerald and mutates into a enormous blob monster that threatens to destroy the planet. Anime Hair: It's very spiky.

characters.name: SwatBots
characters.description: Dr. Robotnik's army of mechanical minions that enforce his rule. Adaptational Wimp: WhileSwatBotsare hardly enormous threats in any incarnation they're shown, they still had some level of threat and had a seemingly minimal chance of breaking or being easily defeated. Here, they're incredibly incompetent and easy to destroy, "Friend or Foe?" has Manic defeat aSWAT Botbytapping it on the head with a drumstick, and "The Pendant" reveals they haveoff switches in their arms. Depending on the Writer: How much of a threat are theSwatBots? Sometimes, they're easy enough to break and don't pose much of a threat, but other times they can catch the heroes off guard and even be formidable adversaries for other freedom fighters. Made of Plasticine: Despite being made of metal,SwatBotsare ludicrously ineffective and have been destroyed by objects like pillows or mattresses falling upon them. Out-of-Character Moment: In "Underground Masquerade", theSwatBotsarrest Manic for stealing coins and not for being part of the Sonic Underground, put him in a regular jail cell instead of immediately roboticizing him, and don't alert Robotnik despite in all other episodes loudly announcing the royal hedgehogs are "priority one". They also prioritize arresting Sleet and Dingo over recapturing Manic once it's clear the that former two were behind the theft.

characters.name: BartlebyMontClair
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He’s often the victim of slapstick comedy. Heel–Face Revolving Door: He refuses to join a side in the conflict between Robotnik and the resistance, mostly doing whatever is best for him at the moment. Upper-Class Twit: He’s a wealthy aristocrat and contributes almost nothing to society.

characters.name: Cyrus
characters.description: The Mole: Robotnik forces him to be one for the sake of his father. He stops after Robotnik betrays him and roboticizes his father.

characters.name: Knuckles the Echidna
characters.description: Knuckles is the guardian of one of the Chaos Emeralds, and lives on the Floating Island. He is a loner who is destined to his duty, but is also well-mannered and believes in the good of people. Advertised Extra: Although he only appeared in four episodes of the seriesnote"Friend or Foe", "Flying Fortress", "No Hedgehog is an Island", and "New Echidna in Town", a good chunk of the series' merchandise was centered around him. He had a puzzle and plush from Denny's, and he was one of the characters featured on the sticker sheet. Aloof Ally: Prefers to work by himself, and in "Chaos Emerald Chaos" the triplets have to sing one of their trademark Aesop-y songs before he's willing to work with them. Big Damn Heroes. Just as Dr. Robotnik was about to roboticize Sonic and his siblings, Knuckles single-handedly saved the triplets, and even destroyed the Portable Roboticizer. Dreadlock Warrior: As always.

characters.name: The Oracle of Delphius
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Population: He's green with spots of darker green. Cartoon Creature: He's supposed to be an aardvark, but the exaggerated flexibility of his snout makes it a little hard to identify. Trickster Mentor: He almost never gives a straight answer, and many of his challenges have hidden meanings or solutions.

characters.name: One-off Characters
characters.description: Defector from Decadence: She and her father join the Resistance once Robotnik's plot is discovered, leaving behind their lavish lifestyle. Lonely at the Top: During the song about how money doesn't buy everything, there's a scene with her sitting alone on a pile of precious gems while other kids are enjoying a good time with their families.
